    background-color: #eeeeee;
    border: 1px solid #cccccc;
    padding: 8px 16px 0 16px;
    font-size: 12px;
}
</style>


TrendAI Vision One™ Public API

## Important
Avoid submitting sensitive information using query parameters.  
Sensitive information included in URLs can be logged or exposed in unwanted locations such as server logs, browser history, and email messages.  
Our APIs use custom request headers (such as `TMV1-*`) in some methods to avoid exposing sensitive information.

get.description:
Displays the notes of the specified Workbench alert in a paginated list
API key role permissions required:
Agentic SIEM and XDR
  • Workbench
    • View, filter, and search

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
Filter for retrieving a subset of Workbench alert notes. Supported fields and operators:

* id - Numeric string that identifies a Workbench alert note
* creatorMailAddress - Email address of the user that created a Workbench alert note
* creatorName - User that created a Workbench alert note
* lastUpdatedBy - Parameter that indicates the user who last modified a Workbench alert note
* 'eq' - Abbreviation of the operator 'equal to'
* 'and' - Operator 'and'
* 'or' - Operator 'or'
* 'not' - Operator 'not'
* '( )' - Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator.

Note: Include this parameter in every request that generates paginated output.

parameters.name: alertId
parameters.description: Unique alphanumeric string that identifies a Workbench alert.

parameters.name: startDateTime
parameters.description:
Timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates the start of the data retrieval time range.

Note: startDateTime cannot be earlier than "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z".

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description:
Timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates the end of the data retrieval time range.

Note: endDateTime cannot be earlier than startDateTime

parameters.name: dateTimeTarget
parameters.description: Parameter that allows you to filter the retrieved Workbench alert notes.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Parameter to be used for sorting records. Records are returned in descending order by default. To return records in ascending order, add a phrase "asc" after the parameter name.
Sub-sorts can be specified by a comma-separated list of property names.
Sortable fields are as follows:
* id
* creatorMailAddress
* creatorName
* lastUpdatedBy
* createdDateTime
* lastUpdatedDateTime

Note: You can use multiple fields, separated by commas, to sort the retrieved Workbench alert notes.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Number of records displayed on a page.

nextLink.description: Link to retrieve the next page of entries in paginated output

error.description: * 3090003 - Unable to process the request. Verify that the request is properly formatted and try again. (Error code: 3090003)

error.description:
* 3090007 - Unable to process the request. Verify that the specified workbench ID exists and that you have permission to access the alert and try again. (Error code: 3090007)
* 3090015 - False Positive Removed. TrendAI™ has identified the alert as a false positive and removed it from Workbench. Please ignore the alert and proceed to other alerts (Error code: 3090015)

message.example: Unable to process the request. Verify that the specified workbench ID exists and that you have permission to access the alert and try again. (Error code: 3090007)

x-code-samples.source:
import requests
import json

url_base = 'https://YOUR_V1_REGION_FQDN'
url_path = '/beta/xdr/workbench/alerts/{alertId}/notes'
url_path = url_path.format(**{'alertId': 'YOUR_ALERTID'})
token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'

query_params = {
    'startDateTime': 'YOUR_STARTDATETIME (string)',
    'endDateTime': 'YOUR_ENDDATETIME (string)',
    'dateTimeTarget': 'YOUR_DATETIMETARGET (string)',
    'orderBy': 'YOUR_ORDERBY (string)',
    'top': 'YOUR_TOP (integer)'
}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'TMV1-Filter': 'YOUR_FILTER (string)'
}

r = requests.get(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

get.description:
Displays information about workbench alerts that match the specified criteria in a paginated list. Note: This method displays up to 10 entries per page. If the response body exceeds 50 MB in size, the method displays only one entry per page.
API key role permissions required:
Agentic SIEM and XDR
  • Workbench
    • View, filter, and search

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
Filter for retrieving a subset of the alert list.

Supported fields:
|Field    |Description |Possible values|
|---------|------------|--------------|
|id |The unique identifier of an alert |Any value|
|investigationStatus **(Deprecated)** |The current status of the Workbench alert or investigation |`New`, `In Progress`, `True Positive`, `False Positive`, `Benign True Positive`, `Closed`|
|status |The status of the case or investigation |`Open`, `In Progress`, `Closed`|
|investigationResult |The findings of the case or investigation |`No Findings`, `Noteworthy`, `True Positive`, `False Positive`, `Benign True Positive`, `Other Findings`|
|alertProvider |Source of a Workbench alert |`SAE`, `TI`|
|modelId |ID of the detection model that triggered the alert |Any value|
|model |The detection model that triggered the alert |Any value|
|modelType |The type of detection model that triggered the alert |`preset`, `custom`|
|severity |The severity assigned to a model that triggered the alert |`critical`, `high`, `medium`, `low`|
|impactScopeEntityValue |Entities affected within the company network |Any value|
|indicatorValue |Objects found using root cause analysis or sweeping |Any value|
|incidentId |The unique identifier of an incident |Any value|

Supported operators:
|Operator |Description |
|---------|------------|
|`eq`|Operator 'equal to'|
|`and`|Operator 'and'|
|`or`|Operator 'or'|
|`not`|Operator 'not'|
|`( )`|Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator.|
|`contains`|Operator that allows you to search for a specified string in a field|

Note: Include this parameter in every request that generates paginated output.

multiCondition.summary: Filters the list by alert status (exact match) and impacted entity (partial match).

impactScopeEntityValue.summary: Filters the list by impacted entity (exact match)

indicatorValue.summary: Filters the list by detected indicator (exact match)

parameters.name: startDateTime
parameters.description: Datetime in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ in UTC) that indicates the start of the data retrieval time range. The available oldest value is "1970-01-01T00:00:00Z"

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description: The datetime in ISO 8601 format that indicates the end of the data retrieval time range.

parameters.name: dateTimeTarget
parameters.description: The timestamp to be used for retrieving Workbench alert data.

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Specifies the field by which the results are sorted. 

Records are returned in descending order by default. To return records in ascending order, add the phrase `asc` after the parameter name.

You can specify multiple fields separated by commas.

Available values:
* id
* caseId
* name
* investigationStatus (deprecated)
* status
* investigationResult
* modelId
* model
* score
* severity
* createdDateTime
* updatedDateTime
* firstInvestigatedDateTime

count.description: Number of workbench alerts displayed on the viewed page.

nextLink.description: Link to retrieve the next page of entries in paginated output.

get.description:
Displays search results from the Detection Data source in a paginated list. The primary purpose of the "Get Detection Data" API is to provide customers with a querying capability, rather than exporting all logs. Important Usage Notes: - A single query can return a maximum of 10,000 logs. If you need to obtain more data, please conduct your queries in batches. - To improve query efficiency and retrieve relevant results, we recommend narrowing your search criteria by adjusting the time range, using specific keywords, or adding filtering conditions. - For large-scale log retrieval, consider using the system's Export function, which allows for batch processing and avoids search result limits.
API key role permissions required:
Agentic SIEM and XDR
  • XDR Data Explorer
    • View queries and Watchlist, and filter and search queries

get.description:
Displays a list of Observed Attack Techniques events that match the specified criteria. > **Note** > > * You cannot retrieve data that is more than 365 days old. > * There are multiple ways to define the retrieval time range: > * Using `detectedStartDateTime + detectedEndDateTime` (Default). > * Using `ingestedStartDateTime + ingestedEndDateTime`. > * Using `detectedStartDateTime + detectedEndDateTime` and `ingestedStartDateTime + ingestedEndDateTime` at the same time. > * If no value is specified, `detectedStartDateTime` and `detectedEndDateTime` use their default values.
API key role permissions required:
Agentic SIEM and XDR
  • Observed Attack Techniques
    • View, filter, and search

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
Filter for retrieving a subset of the collected Observed Attack Techniques events. Include this parameter in every request that generates paginated output.

> **Important**
>
> The name of the `containerName` field might change depending on the products you purchase and the supported products in your region.

Supported fields:
|Field  |Description  |
|---------|---------|
|`uuid`     |The ID of an Observed Attack Techniques event.|
|`riskLevel`     |The severity of a detection. Possible values: `undefined`, `info`, `low`, `medium`, `high`, `critical`|
|`filterName`     | The detection filter that triggered the event |
|`filterMitreTacticId` |The ID of the MITRE ATT&CK tactic associated with an event.|
|`filterMitreTechniqueId` |The ID of the MITRE ATT&CK technique or sub-technique associated with an event.|
|`endpointName` |The name of an endpoint  |
|`agentGuid`     |The ID of the installed agent  |
|`endpointIp`     | The endpoint IP |
|`productCode`     | The Product that generated the alert |
|`containerName`     | The container name |
|`detailCustomAssetTagKey`     | The key of  a custom asset tag associated with an endpoint, including all tag values bound with the key. |
|`detailCustomAssetTagValuesOf`     | The subset of values bound with a custom asset tag key associated with endpoints. Use with `hassubset()` function to filter by specific tag values. |
|`detailPlatformAssetTagKey`     | The key of a platform asset tag associated with an endpoint, including all tag values bound with the key. Note: Only 'Asset group' is available as a key for platform asset tags. |
|`detailPlatformAssetTagValuesOf`     | The subset of values bound with a platform asset tag key associated with endpoints. Use with `hassubset()` function to filter by specific tag values. Note: Only 'Asset group' is available as a key for platform asset tags. |
|`groupId`     | The group to which the endpoint belongs. |

Supported operators:
|Operator|Description|
|-----|-----------|
|`eq`|Operator 'equal to'|
|`and`|Operator 'and'|
|`or`|Operator 'or'|
|`not`|Operator 'not'|
|`()`|Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator|

Additional functions:
|Function|Description|Notes|
|-----|-----------|----------------|
|hassubset()|Checks if the array contains a subset|Applicable to `detailCustomAssetTagValuesOf` and `detailPlatformAssetTagValuesOf` only|

get.description:
Displays all the available packages from a data pipeline a paginated list. > **Note** > > * You need to bind a data type to a pipeline before using this method. > * The data pipeline ingests data at least every 2 minutes. TrendAI™ recommends to get data every 15 minutes. > * Users with custom user roles can only access the data pipelines they are registered to.
API key role permissions required:
Agentic SIEM and XDR
  • XDR Data Explorer
    • View queries and Watchlist, and filter and search queries

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The ID of a data pipeline.

parameters.name: startDateTime
parameters.description:
The start of the data retrieval time range, in ISO 8601 format

You can retrieve data that was saved no earlier than 7 days ago.

Default: 15 minutes before you make the request

> **Tip**
>
> If you have requested data before, TrendAI™ recommends using **latestPackageCreatedDateTime** as the value for **startDateTime** for subsequent requests.

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description:
The end of the data retrieval time range

The difference between 'startDateTime' and 'endDateTime' cannot be greater than `3600` seconds.

Default: The time you make the request.

> **Important**
>
> Due to processing time limitations, ensure "endDateTime" is set to at least 30 seconds before the request execution time.

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Number of records displayed on a page. If no value is specified, 'top' defaults to 500.

id.description: The ID of a package stored in a data pipeline.

createdDateTime.description: The date the data pipeline created the package, in ISO 8601 format

count.description: Number of records displayed on the current page.

totalCount.description: Number of records returned by the request.

requestedDateTime.description: The date and time the pipeline got the current request, in ISO 8601 format.

latestPackageCreatedDateTime.description: The createdDateTime of the last pipeline package produced, in ISO 8601 format.

nextLink.description: Link to retrieve the next page of entries in paginated output.

get.description:
Retrieves information about domains, file SHA-1, file SHA-256, IP addresses, email addresses, or URLs in the Suspicious Object List and displays the information in a paginated list.
API key role permissions required:
THREAT INTELLIGENCE
  • Suspicious Object Management
    • View, filter, and search

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
The parameter that allows you to sort the retrieved search results in ascending or descending order. If no order is specified, the results are shown in ascending order. 

Supported fields and operators:
* 'riskLevel'
* 'lastModifiedDateTime'
* 'expiredDateTime'
* 'asc' - Ascending order
* 'desc' - Descending order

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
The filter for retrieving a subset of the Suspicious Object List. Supported fields and operators:
* 'type' - The type of a suspicious object. Possible values: url, domain, senderMailAddress, ip, fileSha1, fileSha256.
* 'url' - Suspicious URL.
* 'domain' - Suspicious domain name.
* 'ip' - Suspicious IP address.
* 'senderMailAddress' - Suspicious email address.
* 'fileSha1' - SHA1 hash associated to a suspicious file.
* 'fileSha256' - SHA256 hash associated to a suspicious file.
* 'scanAction' - Action that connected products apply after detecting a suspicious object. Possible values: block, log
* 'riskLevel' - Risk level of a suspicious object. Possible values: high, medium, low
* 'eq' - Abbreviation of the operator "equal to"
* 'and' - Operator "and"
* 'or' - Operator "or"
* 'not' - Operator "not"
* '( )' - Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator.

parameters.name: startDateTime
parameters.description: The timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates the start of the data retrieval time range. If no value is specified, 'startDateTime' defaults to the earliest available value for 'lastModifiedDateTime'.

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description: The timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates the end of the data retrieval time range. If no value is specified, 'endDateTime' defaults to the time the request is made.

parameters.name: source
parameters.description: all (default), workbench...

parameters.name: startDateTime
parameters.description:
Start and end time during which logs are to retrieve. Format: ISO 8601 timestamp to the millisecond in UTC, yyyy-mm-ddThh:mm:ss.mmmZ.
If both start and end are not specified, the request retrieves logs within five minutes before the point of time when the request is sent.
If both start and end are specified, the request retrieves logs within the configured duration. Make sure the end time is no earlier than the start time.
If only start is specified, the request retrieves logs within five minutes after the point of the configured start time.
If only end is specified, the request retrieves logs within five minutes before the point of the configured end time.

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description: above

parameters.name: investigationStatus
parameters.description: If All statuses are selected, leave it as null; otherwise, put an array of integer(s); [0]: New; [1]: In Progress; [2]: Resolved: True Positive; [3]: Resolved: False Positive

parameters.name: sortBy
parameters.description: "investigationStatus" / "score" / "wbName" / "wbId" / "createdTime" / "severity"; if it is desc, add a prefix "-" (Ex: "-investigationStatus")

parameters.name: queryTimeField
parameters.description: "updatedTime", "createdTime". The default value is createdTime."

parameters.name: offset
parameters.description: 0-based (0 means the 1st record; 50 means the 51st record)

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description:
The number of records displayed on a page.
The maximum allowed value is 500.

parameters.description:
Filter for retrieving a subset of the Audit Logs list. Supported fields and operators:

* 'loggedUser '- The account that was used to perform the activity.
* 'loggedRole' - Role of the account
* 'accessType' - Source of the activity. Possible values: 'Console', 'API'.
* 'category' - Category of the activity. Possible values: _See Response Code 200 description_.
* 'activity' - The activity that was performed. The 'category' needs to be determined to set this parameter. Possible values: _See Response Code 200 description_.
* 'result' - Outcome of the activity. Possible values: 'Successful', 'Unsuccessful'.
* 'eq' - Abbreviation of the operator "equal to"
* 'and' - Operator "and"
* 'or' - Operator "or"
* 'not' - Operator "not"
* '( )' - Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator.
parameters.example: (category eq 'Account Management') and (activity eq 'Add user account')

parameters.name: startDateTime
parameters.description:
Timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates the start of the data retrieval range. You can retrieve data for response tasks that were created no later than 180 days ago.

If no value is specified, 'startDateTime' defaults to the start of the beginning of the retention period of 180 days.

parameters.name: endDateTime
parameters.description:
Timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates the end of the data retrieval range.

If no value is specified, 'endDateTime' defaults to the time the request is made.

parameters.name: dateTimeTarget
parameters.description:
The timestamp to be used for retrieving the target audit log. 

Available values : loggedDateTime, ingestedDateTime 

Default value : loggedDateTime

parameters.name: orderBy
parameters.description:
Parameter that allows you to sort the retrieved search results in ascending or descending order. If no order is specified, the results are shown in ascending order. Supported fields and operators:
* 'loggedDateTime'
* 'accessType'
* 'ingestedDateTime'
* asc - Ascending order
* desc - Descending order

parameters.name: top
parameters.description: Number of records displayed on a page.

parameters.name: labels
parameters.description:
Parameter that allows you to retrieve the specified elements from the "details" field. Possible values:
* 'none' - Retrieves no elements.
* 'matched' - Retrieves the specified elements in the "details" object.
* 'all" - Retrieves all available elements.

loggedDateTime.description: Timestamp that indicates when an activity occured

ingestedDateTime.description: Timestamp that indicates when the audit log was ingested.

loggedUser.description: The account that was used to perform the activity

category.description:
The category that the performed activity belongs to. 

Example values:

* 'Logon and Logoff'
* 'SAML Single Sign-On'
* 'Account Management'
* 'Product Connector'
* 'Notifications'
* 'Detection Model Management'
* 'Workbench'
* 'Response Management'
* 'Search'

* 'Sandbox Analysis'

* 'ServiceNow'

* 'Case Management'

activity.description:
The activity that was performed. Example values:
* 'Log on' -  Category: Logon and Logoff
* 'Log off' -  Category: Logon and Logoff
* 'Enable single sign-on' -  Category: SAML Single Sign-On
* 'Disable single sign-on' -  Category: SAML Single Sign-on
* 'Add user account' -  Category: Account Management
* 'Delete user account' -  Category: Account Management
* 'Enable user account' -  Category: Account Management
* 'Disable user account' -  Category: Account Management

details.description: Object containing detailed information specific to the audit log activity.  The structure and content vary depending on the category and activity type.

nextLink.description: Link to retrieve the next page of entries in paginated output.

x-code-samples.source:
import requests
import json

url_base = 'https://YOUR_V1_REGION_FQDN'
url_path = '/beta/xdr/audit/logs'
token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'

query_params = {
    'startDateTime': 'YOUR_STARTDATETIME (string)',
    'endDateTime': 'YOUR_ENDDATETIME (string)',
    'dateTimeTarget': 'YOUR_DATETIMETARGET (string)',
    'orderBy': 'YOUR_ORDERBY (string)',
    'top': 'YOUR_TOP (integer)',
    'labels': 'YOUR_LABELS (string)'
}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'Accept': 'YOUR_ACCEPT (string)',
    'TMV1-Filter': 'YOUR_FILTER (string)'
}

r = requests.get(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

get.description:
Displays a list of local and SAML accounts with access to TrendAI Vision One™ in a paginated list Note: This feature is not available for customers that signed up for or expressly updated TrendAI Vision One™.
API key role permissions required:
Administration
  • User Accounts

parameters.name: pageIndex
parameters.description: The page index of user accounts returned in paginated output. This value can be greater than or equal to 1. If you don't use this parameter, ListAccounts returns results of the first page.

parameters.name: pageSize
parameters.description: Number of records displayed on a page. If no value is specified, 'pageSize' defaults to 50.

message.default: Success

type.description:
The type of user account.
* 0 - Custom account
* 1 - AD account

name.description:
The first and last name of the user
This string is the combination of the "firstName" and "lastName" values (with a space in the middle)
Note: When the "lastName" value is not provided but "name" exists, "name" will be used instead.

verificationOverdueTime.description: If the user account is an unvarified local account, this value will be timestamp in seconds when the verification link is expired . If the user account is an AD account or a varified local account, the value will be 0.

400.description: The specified parameter is invalid. Review the available parameters for the API request.

401.description: User account failed to pass the authentication of the server.

message.default: User account Failed to authenticate with the server. Please contact your system administrator.

403.description: User account tries to access resources which he does not have permissions.

message.default: User account has no authorization to access. Please contact your system administrator.

500.description: These errors are usually caused by a server-side issue.

message.default: An internal error has occurred. Please contact TrendAI™ Support.

Modifies the settings of a SAML or local account Note: This feature is not available for customers that signed up for or expressly updated TrendAI Vision One™.
API key role permissions required:
Administration
  • User Accounts
    • Configure account settings
    • View

type.description:
User account type. Supported values:
* 0 - Local account
* 1 - SAML account

name.description:
Obsolete. It is only used when "lastName" does not exist. It is recommended to use "firstName" and "lastName". 
* If "name", "firstName", and "lastName" are provided, only "firstName" and "lastName" are stored by the API.
* If "name" and "firstName" are provided, "name" is stored as "lastName".
* If "name" and "lastName" are provided, "name" is ignored and "firstName" remains empty.

enabled.description:
User account status. Supported values:
* true - Active
* false - Disabled

password.description:
Passwords must be 8 to 25 characters in length and contain at least one 
uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, and one numeric character.

authorization.description:
Access level of the user account. Supported values:
* 1 - TrendAI Vision One™ console only 
* 2 - TrendAI Vision One™ APIs only 
* 3 - TrendAI Vision One™ console and APIs

role.description:

* Operator

x-code-samples.source:
import requests
import json

url_base = 'https://YOUR_V1_REGION_FQDN'
url_path = '/beta/xdr/portal/accounts/{email}'
url_path = url_path.format(**{'email': 'YOUR_EMAIL'})
token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'

query_params = {}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'Content-Type': 'application/json;charset=utf-8'
}
body = {
    'type': 'YOUR_TYPE (integer)',
    'name': 'YOUR_NAME (string)',
    'firstName': 'YOUR_FIRSTNAME (string)',
    'lastName': 'YOUR_LASTNAME (string)',
    'enabled': 'YOUR_ENABLED (boolean)',
    'description': 'YOUR_DESCRIPTION (string)',
    'role': 'YOUR_ROLE (string)'
}

r = requests.put(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers, json=body)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

post.description:
Submits a file to the sandbox for analysis. Note: * You can submit only 1 file per request. For more information about the supported file types, see the [TrendAI Vision One™ Online Help](https://docs.trendmicro.com/en-us/enterprise/trend-micro-xdr-help/SandboxSupportedFiles) * Using Sandbox Analysis requires TrendAI™ Flex.
API key role permissions required:
THREAT INTELLIGENCE
  • Sandbox Analysis
    • Submit object
    • View, filter, and search

documentPassword.description: Password encoded in Base64 used to decrypt the submitted file sample. The maximum password length (without encoding) is 128 bytes.

archivePassword.description: Password encoded in Base64 used to decrypt the submitted archive. The maximum password length (without encoding) is 128 bytes.

arguments.description:
Parameter that allows you to specify Base64-encoded command line arguments to run the submitted file. The maximum argument length before encoding is 1024 bytes. 
Arguments are only available for Portable Executable (PE) files and script files.

Operation-Location.description: Link that you can use to see the status of a submission.

id.description: Unique alphanumeric string that identifies a submission

arguments.description: Command line arguments encoded in Base64 of the submitted file.

message.example: The submitted file size is larger than supported
Rate limit exceeded

post.summary: Export information about CVEs in devices
post.description:
Retrieves information about the highly-exploitable CVEs detected in the devices of your environment. If the request is successful, the API returns a ZIP file containing JSON files with the collected information. > **Important** > > - This is an asynchronous operation. > - Using this API requires allocating TrendAI™ Flex to **Cyber Risk Exposure Management** in TrendAI Vision One™. [Learn more](https://docs.trendmicro.com/en-us/enterprise/trend-micro-xdr-help/ProductsSupportCredits)
API key role permissions required:
Dashboards & Reports
  • Reports
    • Configure and download
    • View

filter.description:
Filter for retrieving a subset of the the highly exploitable CVEs detected in the devices of your environment.

Supported fields:

| Field                        | Description                                                  | Supported values                                                    |
| ---------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `id`                         | The ID of the device on the TrendAI Vision One™ platform     | Any value                                                           |
| `deviceName`                 | The name of the device                                       | Any value                                                           |
| `ip`                         | The IP addresses of a device                                 | Any value                                                           |
| `criticality`                | The criticality of the device                                | `high`, `medium`, `low`                                             |
| `cvssScore`                  | The CVSS score of the detected CVEs                          | Any value                                                           |
| `globalExploitActivityLevel` | Indicates how often attackers are exploiting a vulnerability | `high`, `medium`, `low`                                             |
| `cveId`                      | The Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures identifier (CVE ID) | Any value                                                           |
| `cveMitigationStatus`        | The mitigation status of the CVE detected on the device      | `new`, `inProgress`, `closed`, `dismissed`, `accepted`, `mitigated` |
| `cveEventRiskLevel`          | The risk level of the risk event triggered by a CVE          | `high`, `medium`, `low`                                             |

Supported operators:

| Operator | Description                      |
| -------- | -------------------------------- |
| `eq`     | Operator 'equal to'              |
| `gt`     | Operator 'greater than'          |
| `ge`     | Operator 'greater than or equal' |
| `le`     | Operator 'less than or equal'    |
| `lt`     | Operator 'less than'             |
| `and`    | Operator 'and'                   |
| `or`     | Operator 'or'                    |
| `not`    | Operator 'not'                   |
| `( )`    | Symbols for grouping operands    |

orderBy.description:
The field by which the results are sorted.

To display records in ascending or descending order, add the phrase `asc` or `desc` after the parameter name.

Available values:

* `deviceName`
* `asc` - Ascending order
* `desc` - Descending order

Operation-Location.description:
The URL where you can check the status of the request.

Possible statuses:
- `running`
- `succeeded`
- `failed`

Task results:
- If the request is successful, you can download a ZIP file with the results from the URL in the `resourceLocation` field of the response body.
- If the request fails, the response body contains the error details.

> **Tip**
> For more information, check the documentation for the `/v1.0/asrm/tasks/{id}` endpoint

}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'TMV1-Filter': 'YOUR_FILTER (string)'
}

r = requests.get(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

get.description:
Displays all the highly-exploitable CVEs found in a device in a paginated list. > **Important** > > Using this API requires allocating TrendAI™ Flex to **Cyber Risk Exposure Management** in TrendAI Vision One™. [Learn more](https://docs.trendmicro.com/en-us/enterprise/trend-micro-xdr-help/ProductsSupportCredits)

  • Reports
    • Configure and download

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
The filter for retrieving a subset of the prioritized CVEs. 

Supported fields:

|Field  |Description  |Supported values  |
|---------|---------|---------|
|`id` |The ID of the device on the TrendAI Vision One™ platform. |Any value|
|`deviceName` |The name of the device |Any value|
|`ip` |The IP addresses of a device. |Any value|
|`criticality` |The criticality of the device.|`High`, `Medium`, `Low` |
|`cvssScore` |The CVSS score.|Any value|
|`globalExploitActivityLevel` |The level that indicates how frequently attackers are exploiting a vulnerability.|`High`, `Medium,High` |
|`cveId` |Common Vulnerabilities and Exposures identifier. Also known as CVE ID|Any value|
|`cveMitigationStatus` |The mitigation status of the CVE detected on the device.|`New`, `InProgress`, `Closed`, `Dismissed`, `Accepted`, `Mitigated` |
|`cveEventRiskLevel` | The risk level of the risk event triggered by a CVE.|`High`, `Medium`, `Low` |

Supported operators:

|Operator  |Description  |
|---------|---------|
|`eq`  |Operator 'equal to'. |
|`gt`  |Operator 'greater than'. |
|`ge`  |Operator 'greater than or equal'. |
|`le`  |Operator 'less than or equal'. |
|`lt`  |Operator 'less than'. |
|`and` |Operator 'and'. |
|`or`  |Operator 'or'. |
|`not` |Operator 'not'. |
|`( )` |Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator. |

Displays information about the devices Attack Surface Discovery found in your environment in a paginated list. > **Important** > > Using this API requires allocating TrendAI™ Flex to **Cyber Risk Exposure Management** in TrendAI Vision One™. [Learn more](https://docs.trendmicro.com/en-us/enterprise/trend-micro-xdr-help/ProductsSupportCredits)
API key role permissions required:
Dashboards & Reports
  • Reports

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
Filter for retrieving a subset of the device information list.

Supported fields:

|Field|Description|Supported values|
|-----|-----------|----------------|
|deviceName|Device name|Any value|
|id| The ID of the device on the TrendAI Vision One™ platform.|Any value|
|deviceType| Whether a device can be assessed. You can only include one device type per query| `Can be assessed`, `Cannot be assessed`, `With managed agents`, `Unmanaged device` |
|latestRiskScore|The most recent Risk Score of the device|Any value|
|criticality    |The criticality of the device             | `high`, `medium`, `low` |
|osPlatform     |The operating system of the device        | `Android`, `Linux`, `macOS`, `Windows`, `Other` |
|lastUser       |The last user who signed in to the device|Any value|
|installedAgents|The agents installed on the device| The values in the "installedAgents" field when the request is successful.|
|discoveredBy|The data sources that discovered the device| The values in the "discoveredBy" field when the request is successful.|

Supported operators:

|Operator|Description|Notes|
|-----|-----------|----------------|
|`eq`|Operator 'equal to'| Not applicable to 'discoveredBy' |
|`and`|Operator 'and'| - |
|`or`|Operator 'or'| - |
|`not`|Operator 'not'| - |
|`()`|Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator| - |
|`gt`|Operator 'greater than'|Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore'|
|`ge`|Operator 'greater than or equal'|Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore'|
|`le`|Operator 'less than or equal'|Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore'|
|`lt`|Operator 'less than'|Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore'|

Additional functions:

|Function|Description|Notes|
|-----|-----------|----------------|
|startswith()|Determines if the specified string begins with the specified characters|Only applicable to 'deviceName' and 'lastUser'|
|hassubset()|Checks if the array contains a subset|Applicable to 'discoveredBy', and 'installedAgents' only|

multi-condition.value: (latestRiskScore ge 70) and (installedAgents eq 'Trend Vision One Agent')

discoveredBy.value: hassubset(discoveredBy, ['Trend Micro Deep Security', 'Trend Vision One Agent'])

x-code-samples.source:
import requests
import json

url_base = 'https://YOUR_V1_REGION_FQDN'
url_path = '/beta/xdr/riskInsights/attackSurfaceDevices'
token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'

query_params = {
    'top': 'YOUR_TOP (integer)',
    'lastDetectedStartDateTime': 'YOUR_LASTDETECTEDSTARTDATETIME (string)',
    'lastDetectedEndDateTime': 'YOUR_LASTDETECTEDENDDATETIME (string)',
    'firstSeenStartDateTime': 'YOUR_FIRSTSEENSTARTDATETIME (string)',
    'firstSeenEndDateTime': 'YOUR_FIRSTSEENENDDATETIME (string)'
}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'TMV1-Filter': 'YOUR_FILTER (string)'
}

r = requests.get(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
The filter for retrieving a subset of the local app information list.

Supported fields:

| Field                | Description                                                                  | Supported values                                       |
|----------------------|------------------------------------------------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------|
| id                   | The ID of the local app on the TrendAI Vision One™ platform                  | Any value                                              |
| name                 | The name of the local app                                                    | Any value                                              |
| operatingSystem      | The operating system used by the local application                           | `Windows`, `Linux`, `iOS`, `Android`, `macOS`, `Other` |
| latestRiskScore      | The most recent risk score of the local app                                  | Any value                                              |
| vendor               | The vendor of the local app                                                  | Any value                                              |
| permissionStatus     | The permission status of the local application                               | `allowed`, `blocked`                                   |
| firstSeenDateTime    | The first time Attack Surface Discovery detected the local app               | Any value                                              |
| lastDetectedDateTime | The last time a vulnerability was detected in the local app                  | Any value                                              |

Supported operators:

| Operator | Description                                               | Notes                                                                           |
|----------|-----------------------------------------------------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `eq`     | Operator 'equal to'                                       | -                                                                               |
| `and`    | Operator 'and'                                            | -                                                                               |
| `or`     | Operator 'or'                                             | -                                                                               |
| `not`    | Operator 'not'                                            | -                                                                               |
| `()`     | Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator | -                                                                               |
| `gt`     | Operator 'greater than'                                   | Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore','firstSeenDateTime','lastDetectedDateTime' |
| `ge`     | Operator 'greater than or equal'                          | Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore','firstSeenDateTime','lastDetectedDateTime' |
| `le`     | Operator 'less than or equal'                             | Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore','firstSeenDateTime','lastDetectedDateTime' |
| `lt`     | Operator 'less than'                                      | Only applicable to 'latestRiskScore','firstSeenDateTime','lastDetectedDateTime' |

Additional functions:

| Function   | Description                                       | Notes                       |
|------------|---------------------------------------------------|-----------------------------|
| contains() | Checks if the string contains the specified value | Applicable to `vendor` only |

x-code-samples.source:
import requests
import json

url_base = 'https://YOUR_V1_REGION_FQDN'
url_path = '/beta/cam/gcpProjects'
token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'

query_params = {}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'Content-Type': 'application/json;charset=utf-8'
}
body = {
    'workloadIdentityPoolId': 'YOUR_WORKLOADIDENTITYPOOLID (string)',
    'oidcProviderId': 'YOUR_OIDCPROVIDERID (string)',
    'serviceAccountId': 'YOUR_SERVICEACCOUNTID (string)',
    'projectNumber': 'YOUR_PROJECTNUMBER (string)',
    'name': 'YOUR_NAME (string)',
    'description': 'YOUR_DESCRIPTION (string)',
    'isCAMCloudASRMEnabled': 'YOUR_ISCAMCLOUDASRMENABLED (boolean)'
}

r = requests.post(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers, json=body)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

post.description:
Configures a new Internet Access rule. Note: Newly created rules are listed as the highest priority by default.
API key role permissions required:
ZERO TRUST SECURE ACCESS
  • Secure Access Configuration
    • Configure internet access and AI secure access settings
    • View internet access and AI secure access settings

Location.description: The Internet Access rule was created.

message.example: Invalid parameters. Check the rule structure and try again.

innererror.description:
The detailed error code and message.

  * `SWGNotConfigured` - The TrendAI Vision One™ Internet Access service is not configured. Configure the service and try again.
  * `InvalidRequestBody` - Invalid rule parameters. The rule structure or field types are not valid. Check the parameters and try again.
  * `InvalidValue` - The value in field "xxx" is invalid.
  * `ValueLengthExceed` - The length of the value in field "xxx" exceeds the maximum limit.
  * `EmptySelectedIds` - No user or group selected. Select at least one user or group.
  * `InvalidDefaultGateway` - Invalid gateway. The public or home network cannot be included in the corporate location IDs.
  * `AtLeastOneLocation` - No location selected. Select at least one location.
  * `AtLeastOneURLCategory` - No URL category selected. Select at least one URL category. 
  * `AccessRuleNumExceedsLimit` - The number of rules has reached the maximum of 100. Remove some rules and try again.
  * `AccessRuleNameUnique` - The rule name already exists. Enter a different name and try again.

message.example: Invalid rule parameters. The rule structure or field types are not valid. Check the parameters and try again.

get.description:
Retrieves a non-paginated list of all Internet Access rules. Note: If a field is omitted from the result, the value of that field has been set to the default value.
API key role permissions required:
ZERO TRUST SECURE ACCESS
  • Secure Access Configuration
    • View internet access and AI secure access settings

items.description: The details of all Internet Access rules sorted by priority.

id.description: The resource ID of the Internet Access rule.

patch.description:
Modifies the configuration and priority of a specified Internet Access rule. Note: Only "traffic" and "action" fields can be modified in the default rule. Fields with `null` values are set to the default value.
API key role permissions required:
ZERO TRUST SECURE ACCESS
  • Secure Access Configuration
    • Configure internet access and AI secure access settings
    • View internet access and AI secure access settings

parameters.name: id
parameters.description:
The resource ID of the Internet Access rule.

'C!' is the prefix of the resource ID.

schema.description: The body of the specified Internet Access rule.

priority.description:
The priority of the rule on a scale from 1 (highest) to 100 (lowest).

Default: 1

name.description:
The name for the rule in English or Japanese.

Names should be unique with a maximum length of 128 Unicode characters.

description.description:
The description of the rule in English or Japanese.

Descriptions can be empty or a maximum of 128 Unicode characters.

enabled.description:
The status of the rule.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: Enabled

source.description:
The objects on which the Internet Access rule is applied.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups" "All locations", "Not configured"

target.description:
The target of the Internet Access rule.

When omitted, the field is not updated.    

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups"

userOrGroup.description:
The applicable user or group for the Internet Access rule.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups"

selectedIds.description:
The users or groups to be included in the Internet Access rule.

* `null` - All users/groups
* `not-empty array` - Selected users/groups
* `empty array` - Invalid value

excludedIds.description:
The users or groups to be excluded from the Internet Access rule.

  * `null` - No excluded users/groups
  * `empty array` - No excluded users/groups
  * `not-empty array` - Specified excluded users/group

location.description:
The applicable location for the Internet Access rule.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "All locations"

Note: The corporate location ID may not be set to the ID of the public or home gateway.

If the values for both "corporateLocationIds" and "publicOrHomeNetworks" are set to `empty-array`, the result returns a 400 error.

If the value for "publicOrHomeNetworks" is not set to `empty-array`, the default public or home network is automatically applied.

corporateLocationIds.description:
The applicable cloud and on-premises gateways for the Internet Access rule.

  * `null` - No specified corporate network locations
  * `empty array` - No specified corporate network locations
  * `not-empty array` - Specified corporate network locations

  Note: You can retrieve available gateways using the /beta/ztsa/gateways API.

publicOrHomeNetworks.description:
The applicable public or home network locations for the Internet Access rule.

  * `null` - All public/home network locations
  * `empty` - No public/home network locations
  * `not-empty array` - Invalid value

  Note: Specific public or home network locations are not currently supported.

traffic.description:
The applicable traffic or file type for the Internet Access rule.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "Any traffic", "Any request", "Any file".

urlOrCloudApp.description:
The applicable URLs or cloud apps for the Internet Access rule.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "Any traffic".

Note: If the values of both "predefineUrlCategoryIds" and "customUrlCategoryIds" are set to `empty array` or `null`, the result will return a 400 error if no cloud app categories or actions are configured in the Vision One console.

predefineUrlCategoryIds.description:
The IDs of the applicable predefined URL categories.

  * `null` - No selected predefined URL categories
  * `empty array` - No selected predefined URL categories 
  * `not-empty array` - Specified predefined URL categories

customUrlCategoryIds.description:
The IDs of the applicable custom URL categories for the Internet Access rule.

  * `null` - No specified custom URL categories
  * `empty array` - No specified custom URL categories
  * `not-empty array` - Specified custom URL categories

  Note: You can retrieve the available custom URL categories using the /beta/ztsa/customUrlCategories API.

schedule.description:
The applicable schedule for when the Internet Access rule is applied.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "Always"

startTime.description:
The time the Internet Access rule becomes active.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "00:00"

endTime.description:
The time the Internet Access rule stops being active.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "24:00"

daysOfWeek.description:
The days of the week The Internet Access rule is active.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: ["Monday", "Tuesday", "Wednesday", "Thursday", "Friday"]

period.description:
The status indicating whether to apply the Internet Access rule to only the defined time period.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: Disabled

action.description:
The action to take when the Internet Access rule is applied.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: Access control enabled, advanced security settings disabled

accessControl.description:
The access action taken when the Internet Access rule is applied.

When omitted, the field is not updated.

Default: "Allow cloud app/URL access"

message.example: Invalid parameters. Check the rule structure and try again.

innererror.description:
The detailed error code and message.

 * `SWGNotConfigured` - The TrendAI Vision One™ Internet Access service is not configured. Configure the service and try again.
 * `InvalidRequestBody` - Invalid rule parameters. The rule structure or field types are not valid. Check the parameters and try again.
 * `InvalidValue` - The value in field "xxx" is invalid.
 * `ValueLengthExceed` - The length of the value field "xxx" exceeds the maximum limit.
 * `EmptySelectedIds` - No user or group selected. Select at least one user or group.
 * `InvalidDefaultGateway` - Invalid gateway. The public or home network cannot be included in the corporate location IDs.
 * `AtLeastOneLocation` - No location selected. Select at least one location.
 * `AtLeastOneURLCategory` - No URL category selected. Select at least one URL category.
 * `UpdateDefaultRuleErr` - The value for "xxx" in the default rule cannot be modified.
 * `AccessRuleNumExceedsLimit` - The number of rules has reached the maximum of 100. Remove some rules and try again.
 * `AccessRuleNameUnique` - The rule name already exists. Enter a different name and try again.

message.example: Invalid rule parameters. The rule structure or field types are not valid. Check the parameters and try again.

x-code-samples.source:
import requests
import json

url_base = 'https://YOUR_V1_REGION_FQDN'
url_path = '/beta/cloudPosture/checks'
token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'

query_params = {
    'top': 'YOUR_TOP (integer)',
    'startDateTime': 'YOUR_STARTDATETIME (string)',
    'endDateTime': 'YOUR_ENDDATETIME (string)',
    'dateTimeTarget': 'YOUR_DATETIMETARGET (string)'
}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'TMV1-Filter': 'YOUR_FILTER (string)'
}

r = requests.get(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

post.description:
Creates custom Cloud Risk Management checks with the provided data. The ID of an account check has the following format: `ccc:accountId:ruleId:service:region:resource`. > **Important** > > - You cannot save checks for accounts you do not have access to > - Adding a check with the same `accountId`, `ruleId`, `service`, `region`, and `resource` as an existing one results in a `400` error.
API key role permissions required:

Cloud Risk Management
  • View

parameters.name: accountIds
parameters.description:
A list of Cloud Risk Management account IDs, separated by commas.

> **Important**
>
> - If you provide both `groupId`, and `accountIds`, the precedence order is:
>     1. `groupId`
>     2. `accountIds`
> - If you don't provide any value for `groupId`, or `accountIds`, the API endpoint displays check evolution statistics of the user's organization.

parameters.name: groupId
parameters.description:
The Cloud Risk Management group ID.

> **Important**
>
> - If you provide both `groupId`, and `accountIds`, the precedence order is:
>     1. `groupId`
>     2. `accountIds`
> - If you don't provide any value for `groupId`, or `accountIds`, endpoint displays check evolution statistics of the user's organization.

parameters.name: startDate
parameters.description:
The start of the date range for the compliance evolution statistics, in ISO 8601 format.

Default: 30 days before the request time

> **Important**
>
> If you provide only `startDate`, the date range extends from `startDate` to the present time.

parameters.name: endDate
parameters.description:
The end of the date range for the compliance evolution statistics, in ISO 8601 format.

Default: The request time

> **Important**
>
> If you provide only `endDate`, the date range extends from 30 days before `endDate` to `endDate`.

items.description: Values are based on average number of checks (total, passed, and failed) per day

aggregationDate.description: The date the scores are aggregated, in ISO 8601 format with UTC timezone.

averageCheckCount.description: The average check count for each scan run in a day

newCount.description: The number of failed and successful checks created since the last report date

newFailureCount.description: The number of failed checks created since the last report date

newSuccessCount.description: The number of successful checks created since the last report date

resolvedCount.description: The number of resolved checks since the last report date

knownFailureCount.description: The number of failed checks where failures happened since the last report date

averageSuccessCount.description: The average success check count for each scan run in a day

averageFailureCount.description: The average failure check count for each scan run in a day

post.description:
Re-runs existing checks through a supported communication channel. If you specify an account-level communication configuration, the endpoint returns a `204 (No content)` response. If you specify a company-level communication configuration, the endpoint returns a `207` response. Use this endpoint to send existing check results to a new communication channel. This is a one-time operation as you can forward new checks to communication channels using automatic notifications. Supported communication channels: - Ticketing Systems: - Jira - Zendesk - ServiceNow - Notification Services: - Webhook - AWS SNS > **Important** > > Behavior varies by channel type: > > Ticketing Channels (Jira, Zendesk, ServiceNow): > - Updates existing tickets if check statuses change > - Does not create duplicate tickets > > Notification Channels (Webhooks, AWS SNS): > - Resends all check data with each API call > > Checks might take several minutes for checks to appear in your communication channel following a successful request.
API key role permissions required:
Cloud Risk Management
  • Configure settings
  • View

patch.description:
Updates the specified profile and its associated rule settings. The behavior of the update depends on the `TMV1-Patch-Array-Mode` header and the presence of the `scanRules` field in the request body.
API key role permissions required:
Cloud Risk Management
  • Configure settings
  • View

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: The Cloud Risk Management ID of the profile.

parameters.name: TMV1-Patch-Array-Mode
parameters.description:
The parameter that indicates the behavior of the updates.

The following table describes how the endpoint behaves based on the `TMV1-Patch-Array-Mode` header value and the content of the `scanRules` object in the request body:

| Value                 | Scan rules in Request Body | Behavior                                                                                             |
| --------------------- | -------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `replace`             | Non-empty                  | Adds configured scan rules to an empty profile or overwrites existing scan rules and profile details |
| `replace`             | Empty                      | Retains the profile but clears all scan rules                                                        |
| `update`              | Non-empty                  | Adds/updates only the scan rules passed in the request; other existing scan rules are not affected   |
| `update`              | Empty                      | No changes to the scan rules of the profile                                                          |

post.description:
Applies the specified profile and rule settings to a set of accounts under your organization. > **Important** > > - This is an asynchronous operation. > - After submitting your request, verify the changes by making a GET request to `https://api.xdr.trendmicro.com/beta/cloudPosture/accounts/{id}/scanRules`. > - If you apply the profile to a large number of accounts, wait at least 5 minutes before verifying the changes are applied.
API key role permissions required:

get.summary: Get all real-time posture monitoring events
get.description:
Displays the real-time posture monitoring events you can access in a paginated list. You can get 4 types of events: - `aws`: AWS events - `gcp`: Google Cloud events - `azure`: Azure events - `system`: Cloud Risk Management activity events > **Important** > > - If you don't provide `accountId`, the endpoint returns events from all the accounts you can access. > - You can only access events from accounts you have at least read-only access to.
API key role permissions required:
Cloud Risk Management
  • View

parameters.name: TMV1-Filter
parameters.description:
Filter for retrieving a subset of events. Supported fields:

Field

Description

Supported values

region

The region of the account. Only available for AWS events.

For more information about regions, see the Cloud Risk Management region endpoint

service

The cloud service of the event

For more information about services, see the Cloud Risk Management services endpoint

accountId

The unique Cloud Risk Management identifier of the account.

Any value

provider

The provider of real-time posture monitoring events

  • aws
  • gcp
  • azure
  • system

name

The name of the Cloud Risk Management event

For more information, see the event names table.

eventSourceIdentity

The identifier of the user that caused the event.

Only incoming AWS, Azure, and Google Cloud events haveeventSourceIdentity.

Any value
| Operator | Description | Usage | | ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `eq` | Operator 'equal to' | `service eq 'CloudFormation'` | | `and` | Operator 'and' | `service eq 'CloudFormation' and name eq 'account.monitoring.activity'` | | `or` | Operator 'or' that can only be used in `()` | `(region eq 'us-west-1' or region eq 'us-west-2') and service eq 'CloudFormation'` | | `not` | Operator 'not' | `service eq 'CloudFormation' and not (name eq 'account.monitoring.activity')` | | `( )` | Operator to group filters for precedence | `(service eq 'CloudFormation' or service eq 'S3') and region eq 'us-west-2` | | `contains` | Operator that allows you to search for a specified string in a field (partial match). Support fields: `name`, `eventSourceIdentity` | `contains(name, 'account.scan')` | Event names:

x-code-samples.source:
import requests
import json

url_base = 'https://YOUR_V1_REGION_FQDN'
url_path = '/beta/cloudPosture/reportConfigurations'
token = 'YOUR_TOKEN'

query_params = {}
headers = {
    'Authorization': 'Bearer ' + token,
    'Content-Type': 'application/json;charset=utf-8'
}
body = {
    'accountId': 'YOUR_ACCOUNTID (string)',
    'reportTitle': 'YOUR_REPORTTITLE (string)',
    'includeChecks': 'YOUR_INCLUDECHECKS (boolean)',
    'emailRecipients': 'YOUR_EMAILRECIPIENTS (array)',
    'language': 'YOUR_LANGUAGE (string)',
    'reportType': 'YOUR_REPORTTYPE (string)',
    'checksFilter': {
        'categories': 'YOUR_CATEGORIES (array)',
        'tags': 'YOUR_TAGS (array)',
        'description': 'YOUR_DESCRIPTION (string)',
        'newerThanDays': 'YOUR_NEWERTHANDAYS (integer)',
        'olderThanDays': 'YOUR_OLDERTHANDAYS (integer)',
        'providers': 'YOUR_PROVIDERS (array)',
        'regions': 'YOUR_REGIONS (array)',
        'resourceId': 'YOUR_RESOURCEID (string)',
        'resourceSearchMode': 'YOUR_RESOURCESEARCHMODE (string)',
        'resourceTypes': 'YOUR_RESOURCETYPES (array)',
        'riskLevels': 'YOUR_RISKLEVELS (array)',
        'ruleIds': 'YOUR_RULEIDS (array)',
        'services': 'YOUR_SERVICES (array)',
        'statuses': 'YOUR_STATUSES (array)',
        'suppressed': 'YOUR_SUPPRESSED (boolean)',
        'complianceStandardIds': 'YOUR_COMPLIANCESTANDARDIDS (array)'
    },
    'schedule': {
        'frequency': 'YOUR_FREQUENCY (string)',
        'timezone': 'YOUR_TIMEZONE (string)'
    },
    'reportFormatsInEmail': 'YOUR_REPORTFORMATSINEMAIL (array)'
}

r = requests.post(url_base + url_path, params=query_params, headers=headers, json=body)

print(r.status_code)
for k, v in r.headers.items():
    print(f'{k}: {v}')
print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):
    print(json.dumps(r.json(), indent=4))
else:
    print(r.text)

        'newerThanDays': 'YOUR_NEWERTHANDAYS (integer)',

}

for k, v in r.headers.items():

print('')
if 'application/json' in r.headers.get('Content-Type', '') and len(r.content):

else:

If the rule finds no matching resources based on the input, the endpoint returns an empty array (`[]`). > **Important** > > - Use this endpoint to develop and test custom rules only. > - This endpoint restricted to users with the *Master Administrator* role. > - Executing a rule via this endpoint only returns results or related data and **does not** affect saved data or trigger events (e.g., checks, notifications, reports). > - When testing against a cloud account, the resource data used reflects the state at the time of the last account scan, not necessarily the live state.

|`id`|The unique identifier of a storage container|Any value|
|`provider`|The cloud storage provider| aws |
|`account`|The cloud storage provider account| Any Value |
|`name`|The name of the storage container|Any value|
|`status`|The protection status of the storage container| `protected`, `notProtected`, `notDeployed` |
|`scannedDateTime`|The scan time of the storage container| Any value |

Supported operators:

| Operator | Description                      | Notes                                |
| -------- | -------------------------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `eq`     | Operator 'equal to'              | -                                    |
| `and`    | Operator 'and'                   | -                                    |
| `or`     | Operator 'or'                    | -                                    |
| `not`    | Operator 'not'                   | -                                    |
| `( )`    | Symbols for grouping operands    | -                                    |
| `gt`     | Operator 'greater than'          | Only applicable to `scannedDateTime` |
| `ge`     | Operator 'greater than or equal' | Only applicable to `scannedDateTime` |
| `le`     | Operator 'less than or equal'    | Only applicable to `scannedDateTime` |
| `lt`     | Operator 'less than'             | Only applicable to `scannedDateTime` |

Additional functions:

createdDateTime.description: The date and time the alert was created in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ, UTC).

updatedDateTime.description: The date and time the alert was last updated in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ, UTC).

firstInvestigatedDateTime.description: The date and time the case status was changed to 'In progress' in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ, UTC).

incidentId.description: The incident ID which is the resource ID returned from the insight API.

caseId.description: The unique identifier of a case.

ownerIds.description: The owners of the Workbench alert

modelId.description: ID of the detection model that triggered the alert

description.description: Description of the detection model that triggered the alert
description.example: A backdoor was possibly implanted after a user received a possible spear phishing email message.

indicators.description: The indicators refer to those objects which are found by RCA or sweeping

type.description: Indicator type

field.description: The detailed description of the indicator

value.description: The indicator value

items.description:
Parameter that indicates the origin of a workbench alert's indicator.

Note: If provenance is set to 'Alert', the indicator is considered the "patient zero" of a workbench alert.

matchedDateTime.description: Datetime in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ in UTC)
Datetime in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ in UTC)

type.description:
Prefix that indicates the data source of a field. Possible values:
  * TELEMETRY_ : Endpoint activity data
  * PRODUCT_ : Detections
  * JAGUAR_ : Detections
  * MESSAGE_ : Message activity data

modelId.description: ID of the detection model that triggered the alert

campaign.description: An object-ref to a campaign object

regionAndCountry.description:
region/country
- The region field would follow the STIX2.1 standard
- The country field would follow the Alpha-2 standard
- If only region or country is provided, the slash would be removed

reportLink.description: The URL of the detailed analysis report. For TrendAI™ research reports, the URL points to TrendAI™ blog.

incidentId.description: The incident ID which is the resource ID returned from the insight API.

indicators.description: The indicators refer to those objects which are found by RCA or sweeping

type.description: Indicator type

fields.description: Array that contains detailed information about an indicator.

items.description: Detailed description of the indicator

value.description: Indicator value

firstSeenDateTimes.description: First seen date times from related entities, datetime in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ in UTC)

lastSeenDateTimes.description: Last seen date times from related entities, datetime in ISO 8601 format (yyyy-MM-ddThh:mm:ssZ in UTC)

items.description:
Parameter that indicates the origin of a workbench alert's indicator.

Note: If provenance is set to 'Alert', the indicator is considered the "patient zero" of a workbench alert.

pattern.description:
STIX indicator will be a pattern. The pattern may include "and/or" operation, or relationship, e.g. FOLLOWEDBY, START/STOP.
* ([ipv4-addr:value = '198.51.100.5'] FOLLOWEDBY [ipv4-addr:value = '198.51.100.10'])
* [file:name = 'foo.dll'] START '2016-06-01T00:00:00Z' STOP '2016-07-01T00:00:00Z'

tags.description: Tags defined by STIX

name.description: Host name

id.description: Numeric string that identifies a Workbench alert note

content.description: Content of the Workbench alert note

creatorMailAddress.description: Email address of the user that created the Workbench alert note

creatorName.description: User that created the Workbench alert note

createdDateTime.description: Timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates when the Workbench alert note was created.

lastUpdatedBy.description: Parameter that indicates the user who last modified the note

lastUpdatedDateTime.description: Timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates when the note was last updated.

entityValue.description:
The affected entities within the company network.

Note: 'entityValue' is an object when the value of 'entity' is "host"; string otherwise.

allOf.description: This field is only applicable when the value of 'entityType' is `host`

oneOf.description: This field is only applicable when the value of 'entityType' is `account`, `emailAddress`, `container`, `cloudIdentity` or `cloudWorkload`

entityId.description:
The identifier of an entity.

Supported values:
  * The value of any of the following fields: `account`, `emailAddress`, `container`, `cloudIdentity` or `cloudWorkload`.
  * The primary key of `hostNode` (GUID > ips > name).

oneOf.description: The entity ID id may be a set of IPs for some host-type entities.

items.description:
The origin of a Workbench alert's impact scope.

Note: If 'provenance' is set to 'Alert', the impact scope is considered the "patient zero" of a Workbench alert.

managementScopeInstanceId.description: The instance ID for asset visibility scope

managementScopePartitionKey.description: The partition key for asset visibility scope

ase-startDateTime.description: The start of the data retrieval range in ISO 8601 format. Default:startDateTime defaults to 24 hours before the request is made.

ase-endDateTime.description: Timestamp in ISO 8601 format that indicates the end of the data retrieval time range. If no value is specified, 'endDateTime' defaults to the time the request is made.

ase-top.description: The number of records displayed on a page in default mode.

totalCount.description: The number of records returned by a query.

ase-emailActivityDataV3.description: The Email and Collaboration Activity Data source. Note: For more information, see [https://github.com/trendmicro/tm-v1-schema/blob/master/doc/Email%20Activity%20Data.yaml](https://github.com/trendmicro/tm-v1-schema/blob/master/doc/Email%20Activity%20Data.yaml).

nextLink.description:
Link to retrieve the next page of entries in paginated output. 

Note: Include this parameter in every request that generates paginated output.

progressRate.description: Indicates the percentage of data from the data source that has been already processed. This number is updated every time you retrieve the next page of entries.

msgUuid.description: Unique ID of the email message

mailbox.description: Mailbox where the email message is

mailSenderIp.description: Source IP address of the email message

mailFromAddresses.description: Sender email address of the email message

mailWholeHeader.description: Information about the header of the email message

mailToAddresses.description: A list of recipient email addresses of the email message

mailSourceDomain.description: Source domain of the email message

org_id.description: Unique ID used to identify an organization

arp-taskStatus.description:
The status of the command sent to the managing server. 

Possible task statuses:
- `queued `: The managing server queued the command because the agent was offline or due to a high volume of requests.
- `running`: TrendAI Vision One™ sent the command to the managing server and is waiting for a response
- `succeeded`: The managing server successfully received the command
- `failed`: An error or time-out occurred when attempting to send the command to the managing server
- `canceled`: The task was manually terminated
- `pendingApproval`: The task is pending approval from specified users
- `rejected`: The task was rejected

cam-innerErrorResponse.description: An object representing an internal server error response.

code.description:
The error code and description.

Available values:
- `invalid-feature`: The requested feature does not exist.
- `assume-identity-failed`: The identity provided in the request cannot be assumed. This typically indicates an issue with the customer's deployment.
- `invalid-request-header`: Some of the request headers are either missing or invalid.
- `invalid-request-body`: The structure of the request body is invalid and does not match the expected format.
- `invalid-parameter`: Some of the request parameters are either missing or invalid.
- `missing-role-tag`: The role associated with the provided `roleArn` does not have the 'VisionOneCloudFormationStackName' role tag.

name.description:
The English or Japanese name of the Internet Access rule.

Names must be unique with a maximum length of 128 Unicode characters.

description.description:
The English or Japanese description of the Internet Access rule.

The maximum length of a description is 128 Unicode characters.
description.example: This is an example of a request body.

enabled.description: The status indicating whether the Internet Access rule is enabled.

source.description:
The objects on which the Internet Access rule is applied.

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups", "All locations"

target.description:
The target of the Internet Access rule.

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups"

userOrGroup.description:
The applicable users or groups for the Internet Access rule.

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups"

location.description:
The applicable location for the Internet Access rule.

Default: "All locations"

The corporate location ID may not be set to the ID of the public or home gateway.

If the values for both "corporateLocationIds" and "publicOrHomeNetworks" are set to `empty-array`, the result returns a 400 error.

If the value for "publicOrHomeNetworks" is not set to `empty-array`, the default public or home network is automatically applied.

traffic.description:
The applicable traffic type for the Internet Access rule.

Default: "Any traffic"

urlOrCloudApp.description:
The applicable URLs or cloud apps for the Internet Access rule.

Default: "Any traffic"

Note: If the values of both "predefineUrlCategoryIds" and "customUrlCategoryIds" are set to `empty array` or default, the result will return a 400 error.

schedule.description:
The applicable schedule for when the Internet Access rule is applied.

Default: "Always"

period.description:
The status indicating whether to apply the Internet Access rule only during the specified time period.

Default: Disabled

type.description: The specified user or group.

value.description: The unique identifier of the user or group.

sig-selectedIds.description:

sig-startTime.description:
The time of day the Internet Access rule becomes active.

Valid start times are every half hour from 00:00 to 23:30. Start times must be earlier than end times.

sig-endTime.description:
The time of day the Internet Access rule stops being active.

Valid end times are every half hour from 00:30 to 24:00. End times must be later than start times.

sig-daysOfWeek.description: The days of the week the Internet Access rule is active.

startDate.description: The period of time the Internet Access rule is active.

endDate.description: The date the Internet Access rule stops being active.

sig-schedule.description:
The schedule during which the Internet Access rule is active.

Default: "Always"

sig-priority.description: The priority of the Internet Access rule.

sig-name.description: The name of the Internet Access rule.

sig-description.example: this is an example of request body
sig-description.description: The description of the Internet Access rule.

sig-enabled.description: The status indicating whether the Internet Access rule is enabled.

type.description: The user or group.

id.description: The unique identifier of the user or group.

sig-selectedItems.description:
The users or groups included in the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - All users/groups
  * `not-empty array` - Selected users/groups

sig-excludedItems.description:
The users or groups excluded from the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No excluded users/groups
  * `not-empty array` - Specified excluded users/groups

id.description: The resource IDs.

name.description: The resource names.

id.description: The resource ID.

name.description: The resource name.

sig-privateIpGroup.description:
The private IP address group.

Note: The private IP value can only be configured within the Vision One console.

selectedItems.description:
The private IP addresses included in the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - All private IP address groups
  * `not-empty array` - Selected private IP address groups

excludedItems.description:
The private IP address groups excluded from the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No excluded private IP address groups
  * `not-empty array` - Specified excluded private IP address groups

sig-corporateLocationsGet.description:
The applicable cloud and on-premises gateways for the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No specified corporate network locations
  * `not-empty array` - Specified corporate network locations

name.description: The resource name of the region or public IP address group.

id.description:
The resource ID of the public IP address group.

Note: The ID field only exists for rules including public IP address groups.

sig-source.description:
The objects on which the Internet Access rule is applied.

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups", "Not configured", "All locations"

target.description:
The target of the Internet Access rule.

Default: "All users/groups", "No excluded users/groups"

deviceProfile.description:
The device profile.

Default: "Not configured"

Note: The device profile field can only be configured in the Vision One console.

location.description:
The applicable location for the Internet Access rule.

Default: "All locations"

publicOrHomeNetworks.description:
The applicable public or home networks for the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - All public/home network locations
  * `not-empty array` - Any selected public/home network locations
  * `empty array` - No public/home network locations

  Note: public or home network locations can only be configured in the Vision One console.

sig-traffic.description:
The traffic or file type associated with the Internet Access rule.

Default: "Any traffic", "Any request", "Any file"

urlOrCloudApp.description:
The URL or cloud app associated with the Internet Access rule.

Default: "Any traffic"

predefineUrlCategories.description:
The predefined URL categories associated with the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No selected predefined URL categories 
  * `not-empty array` - Specified predefined URL categories

  Note: For details on available predefined URL categories, see the URL category table in POST.

customUrlCategories.description:
The custom URL categories associated with the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No selected custom URL categories
  * `not-empty array` - Specified custom URL categories

customCloudAppCategories.description:
The custom cloud app categories associated with the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No selected custom cloud app categories
  * `not-empty array` - Specified custom cloud app categories

  Note: Custom cloud app categories can only be configured in the Vision One console.
