The predefined cloud app categories associated with the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No selected predefined cloud app categories
  * `not-empty array` - Specified predefined cloud app categories

  Note: Predefined cloud app categories can only be configured in the Vision One console.

The applicable actions performed when accessing custom cloud apps under the Internet Access rule.

  * `omitted` - No selected custom cloud app actions
  * `not-empty array` - Specified custom cloud app actions

  Note: Custom cloud app actions can only be configured in the Vision One console.

httpOrHttpsRequestFilters.description:
The HTTP or HTTPS request filters associated with the Internet Access rule.

Default: "Any request"

Note: HTTP or HTTPS filters can only be configured in the Vision One console.

fileProfile.description:
The file profile associated with the Internet Access rule.

Default: "Any file"

Note: File profiles can only be configured in the Vision One console.

sig-accessControl.description: The access control action associated with the Internet Access rule.

tenancyRestrictions.description:
The tenancy restriction rule.

Note: The tenancy restriction rule field only exists when tenancy restriction is enabled.

Tenancy restrictions can only be configured in the Vision One console.

threatProtections.description:
The threat protection profile or rule.

Note: The threat protection field only exists when threat protection is enabled.

Threat protection profiles and rules can only be configured in the Vision One console.

dataLossPreventions.description:
The Data Loss Prevention rule.

Note: The Data Loss Prevention field only exists when Data Loss Prevention is enabled.

Data Loss Prevention rules can only be configured in the Vision One console.

sig-nextPaginationResponseField.description: The link to retrieve the next page of entries in paginated output.

name.description:
The name of the custom URL category.

Names must be unique with a maximum of 128 Unicode characters.

items.description:
The entries within the custom URL category.

The maximum number of entries is 1000. Duplicate entries are deleted.

matchMode.description:
The match mode used for entries in the custom URL category.

  * `domain` - Matches the specified domain and corresponding subdomains
  * `keyword` - Matches URLs containing the specified keyword
  * `url` - Matches specified URLs

value.description:
The value for a specified custom URL category entry.

The maximum length of an entry value is 1024 Unicode characters.

error.description: The error message

message.example: User is not authorized to access this resource with an explicit deny

innererror.description: App specific errors beyond the root-level code.

message.example: Unauthorized

apm-extraSettingsChoiceSingleValueType.title: Single Choice Value
apm-extraSettingsChoiceSingleValueType.description:
Used when a single value should be selected from multiple choices.

Allowed values:
- For Support-001 (Support Plan)
  - `Basic`
  - `Developer`
  - `Business`
  - `Enterprise`
- For EC2-025 (EC2 Instance Tenancy)
  - `default`
  - `dedicated`
  - `host`
- For ELB-008 (ELB Listener Security)
  - `1` (Yes)
  - `0` (No)
- For ELB-010, ELBv2-004 (ELB/ELBv2 Minimum Number Of EC2 Instances)
  - Minimum Number Of EC2 Instances
    - 1 (One instance)
    - 2 (Two instances)
- For ELBv2-005 (ELBv2 ALB Listener Security)
  - Include Internal Load Balancers
    - `1` (Yes)
    - `0` (No)

Determines how the profile is applied to accounts. 

Supported values:

- `fill-gaps`: Merges profile settings with existing account settings. In case of conflicts, existing account settings take precedence.
- `overwrite`: Merges profile settings with existing account settings. In case of conflicts, profile settings take precedence.
- `replace`: Clears all existing account settings and applies settings from this profile.

includeChecks.description:
Whether to include checks in PDF reports.

> **Important**
> 
> If the total number of checks exceeds 10,000, not all checks are included in the report.

emailRecipients.description: The list of report recipient email addresses.

apm-reportConfigFilterBase.description:
The conditions for filtering checks that appear in the reports, entered in the `checksFilter` field.

> **Tip**
>
> - To combine single conditions in a field, use the AND operator.
> - To combine multiple conditions in a field, use the OR operator.

description.description: The filter for including checks in the report based on the description of a check.

newerThanDays.description:
The filter for specifying how recent checks should be to be included in the report. 

> **Tip**
> - Set `newerThanDays` to 5 to include checks from the last 5 days. 
> - Leave `olderThanDays` empty to view checks up to the day you make the request.

olderThanDays.description:
The filter for specifying how old checks should be to be included in the report. 

> **Tip**
>
> - Set `olderThanDays` to 5 to include all checks older than 5 days. 
> - Leave `newerThanDays` empty to view checks that occurred before the specified period.

resourceId.description: The filter for including checks based on resource ID.

resourceSearchMode.description:
The search mode for the resource filter.

Exact match or wildcards (`*`) are supported.

resourceTypes.description:
The specified resource types that determine which checks to include in the report.

For a list of resource types, see [Cloud Risk Management resource types](https://conformity.us-1.cloudone.trendmicro.com/v1/resource-types).

riskLevels.description: The specified risk levels that determine which checks to include in the report.

ruleIds.description:
The specified ruleIDs that determine which checks to include in the report.

For a list of ruleIDs, see  [Cloud Risk Management rule IDs](#tag/Services/paths/~1beta~1cloudPosture~1services/get).

statuses.description: The specified statuses that determine which checks to include in a report. Each status indicates a check result.

suppressed.description:
Whether to include suppressed or regular checks only. 

Default: Both suppressed and regular checks are included in the report.

frequency.description:
The report schedule configuration represented as a cron expression.

The system automatically selects hour and minute values. You can only specify the day of the month, day of the week, and month.

Supported format: `(day of the month) (month) (day of the week)`

Allowed values:
- `*`: Every occurrence
- `,`: Separator. For example: `1,4` means first and fourth occurrences
- `-`: Range. For example: `1-4` means first through fourth occurrences
- `/`: Step values. For example: `*/3` means every third occurence

Example: `* * 2` means `Every Tuesday`

enabled.description: Whether the report is scheduled

Note: Single conditions in a field are combined using the AND operator. Multiple conditions in a field are combined using the OR operator.

riskLevels.description: A list of valid risk levels

tags.description:

Example: tags ["dev"] matches the resource with the tag \"environment::dev\" in the filter

communicationConfigurationId.description:
The ID of the communication configuration

> **Important**
>
> If you specify an account-level communication configuration, the endpoint returns a `204 (No content)` response. If you specify a company-level communication configuration, the endpoint returns a `207` response.

accountIds.description: The target Account IDs to Re-run checks for.

startDateTime.description:
The start of the check data retrieval range, in ISO 8601 format.

The start time must not be earlier than `endDateTime`.

You can use either the date when the check was created and assigned a status or the date the status changed.

Tip: To re-run all checks from a particular day up to today, pass in `startDateTime` and omit `endDateTime`.

endDateTime.description:
The end of the check data retrieval range, in ISO 8601 format.

You can use either the date when the check was created and assigned a status or the date the status changed.

Tip: To re-run all checks from a particular day up to today, pass in `startDateTime` and omit `endDateTime`.

You can use either the date when the check was created and assigned a status or the date the status changed.

Tip: To re-run all checks from a particular day up to today, pass in `startDateTime` and omit `endDateTime`.

endDateTime.description:
The end of the check data retrieval range, in ISO 8601 format.

You can use either the date when the check was created and assigned a status or the date the status changed.

Tip: To re-run all checks from a particular day up to today, pass in `startDateTime` and omit `endDateTime`.

operator.description:
The operator to evaluate the input of the condition.
Available operators:
| Category | Operator |
|----------|----------|
|  Regex   |`pattern`|
|  String  |`equal`, `notEqual`, `lessThan`, `lessThanInclusive`, `greaterThan`, `greaterThanInclusive`|
|  Array   |`in`, `notIn`, `contains`, `doesNotContain`|
|  Nullish |`isNullOrUndefined`|
|  Date    |`dateComparison`|

The type of data returned by the query results ("default" displays all data returned by the query, "countOnly" displays the number of records returned, and "performance" limits the returned records to 500.):
* 'default' - Displays the data returned by the query
* 'countOnly' - Displays the number of records returned by the query
* 'performance' - 'performance' - Displays the data in performance mode, but the "top" parameter does not work and there may be zero records returned even though progressRate has not reached 100. To display all records, call the API again via nextLink until progressRate has reached 100. Use count mode to avoid returning empty data.

cam-aws-tmv1-filter.description:
The filter for retrieving a list of a subset of connected AWS accounts.

Supported fields:
|Field  |Description |Supported values|
|---------|---------|---------|
|name|The name of the AWS account.|Any value|
|state|The state of the AWS account.|`managed`, `outdated`, `failed`|
|featureId|The features enabled for the AWS account.|`container-security`, `cloud-response`|
|id|The ID of the AWS account.|Any value|

Supported operators:
|Operator  |Description  |
|---------|---------|
|`eq`|Operator 'equal to'|
|`and`|Operator 'and'|
|`or`|Operator 'or'|
|`not`|Operator 'not'|
|`( )`|Symbols for grouping operands with their correct operator.|
|`contains`|Operator that allows you to search for a specified string in a field|

Note: Include this parameter in every request that generates paginated output.

conditionExpress1.summary: List AWS accounts with states of 'managed' or 'outdated'.

conditionExpress2.summary: List managed AWS accounts with names containing 'lab' or IDs containing '123'.

apm-checksFilter.name: TMV1-Filter
apm-checksFilter.description:
The filter for retrieving a subset of the Cloud Risk Management checks. Supported fields:
FieldDescriptionSupported values
accountIdThe Cloud Risk Management IDs

  • Single account ID:accountId eq '3c8f0d33-65f0-4802-97f3-4475bb70e43e'

  • multiple account IDs:(accountId eq '9d4kd94l-d839-3932-88d0-x9839d85jfp0' or accountId eq '38d92k21-3821-d829-s823-38d920s84kfi')

regionThe region of the accountFor more information about regions, see the Cloud Risk Management region endpoint
serviceThe cloud service of the check to filter on.For more information about services, see the Cloud Risk Management service endpoint
categoriesA list of categories of the check.
  • security
  • cost-optimisation
  • reliability
  • performance-efficiency
  • operational-excellence
  • sustainability

Example:['sustainability', 'performance-efficiency']

riskLevelThe risk level of the check
  • LOW
  • MEDIUM
  • HIGH
  • VERY_HIGH
  • EXTREME
statusThe status of the checkSUCCESS,FAILURE
ruleIdThe rule IDs of checks to be returnedAny value
resourceThe resource IDAny value
descriptionThe check descriptionAny value
suppressed

Whether the check is suppressed

Default: All checks

true,false
tagsThe tags associated with a cloud resourceAny value
complianceStandardIdsA list of supported standard or framework IDs
  • AWAF
  • AZUREWAF-2024
  • GCPWAF

| Operator | Description | Supported fields | Example | | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | | `eq` | Operator 'equal to' | All | `service eq 'EC2'` | | `and` | Operator 'and' | All | `service eq 'EC2' and riskLevels eq 'HIGH'` | | `or` | Operator 'or' that can only be used in `( )` | All | `(service eq 'EC2' or service eq 'S3') and riskLevels eq 'HIGH'` | | `not` | Operator 'not' | All | `service eq 'EC2' and not (riskLevels eq 'HIGH')` | | `( )` | Operator to group filters for precedence. | All | `(service eq 'EC2' or service eq 'S3')` | | `contains` | Operator to check if a string contains the specified string. Supports partial matching. | `service`, `description`, `ruleId`, `resource` | `contains(description, 'Role IAM') and contains(ruleId, 'S3')` | | `hassubset` | Operator to check if an array contains the specified elements. Supports partial matching. | `categories` | `hassubset(categories, ['security', 'reliability'])` | | `any` | Operator to filter tags. You can use the `eq` and `contains` operators to filter tags. | `tags`, `complianceStandardIds` | `tags/any(tag: tag eq 'Environment::development')`, `tags/any(tag: contains(tag, 'Service'))`, `complianceStandards/any(o: o.id in ['GDPR', 'PCI'])` | > **Important** > > The `and` operator is not supported inside parentheses.

apm-startDateTime.name: startDateTime
apm-startDateTime.description:
The start of the data retrieval range in ISO 8601 format. 

Default: Retrieves all checks from the earliest available data.

apm-endDateTime.name: endDateTime
apm-endDateTime.description:
The end of the data retrieval range in ISO 8601 format.

Default: The time you make the request

apm-dateTimeTarget.name: dateTimeTarget
apm-dateTimeTarget.description:
The target field used to filter based on the values provided in either `startDateTime` or `endDateTime` 

Supported fields: `createdDate`
